farms_affected_n: 14.2 million yellowtail killed; worst Chattonella event on record
desalination_affected: ~71M USD at 1972 exchange rates (~300 JPY/USD); some sources cite ~90M USD
verification_status: First major documented fish-killing HAB in Japan; triggered extensive research program

species_notes: Formerly Chrysochromulina polylepis; transferred to Prymnesium
fish_mortality_notes: Killed farmed and wild fish, plus massive invertebrate mortality
ecological_notes: Massive mortality across trophic levels; ecosystem-disruptive event; 'Silent Spring in the Sea'
economic_notes: Fish farm losses; broader ecosystem costs not quantified
notes: Triggered major research effort on haptophyte toxicity; first recognized ecosystem-wide HAB impact in Scandinavia

verification_status: First documented C. leadbeateri fish-killing event; same species caused 2019 and 2025 events

economic_notes: First major P. farcimen event in Norway
notes: Early spring bloom; P. farcimen thrives at low temperatures

species_notes: A. catenella co-occurred causing PSP closures
fish_mortality_notes: Massive die-off required offshore dumping of carcasses
human_health_notes: PSP closures due to co-occurring A. catenella
ecological_notes: Massive nutrient release from decomposing salmon
economic_notes: Includes direct mortality + downstream economic impacts; some estimates higher
mitigation_methods: airlift pumping, cage towing, delayed seeding
response_notes: Airlift pumping possibly counterproductive (lysed fragile cells)
notes: Largest farmed fish HAB mortality ever recorded globally; triggered major industry restructuring

water_body: Gulf of Mexico, Sarasota Bay, Tampa Bay
fish_species_affected: Multiple species including snook, tarpon, manatees
fish_mortality_notes: Fifth longest red tide since 1954; massive fish kills on beaches
human_health_notes: Widespread respiratory irritation from aerosolized brevetoxins
mitigation_methods: Tourism and cleanup costs substantial but not systematically quantified
column73: Affected SW NW and E coasts simultaneously; recurrent annual phenomenon

species_notes: Same species as 1991 event; cold-water ribotype
economic_notes: 2.3-2.9 billion NOK; includes direct + indirect losses
mitigation_methods: emergency harvest, early warning, cage sinking
notes: Largest HAB fish kill in Northern Europe history at the time; continuous spreading bloom unlike 2025

species_notes: P. pouchetii dominant early; C. leadbeateri increasing through event
fish_mortality_notes: 401,794 fish (39.47%) mortality at Fornes site; ~8M total regional
column72: Series of local blooms unlike 2019 continuous bloom; ~18 farms affected; dual-species event

species_notes: First H. akashiwo event in Chile
human_cases: Loss of 50% of national fish production at the time
mitigation_attempted: 50% of Chile national fish production lost
column72: First HAB fish-killing event in Chilean salmon industry; 100,000 cells/mL max density

event_name: 1989 Big Glory Bay Heterosigma salmon kill
species_notes: First record of Heterosigma in New Zealand
human_deaths: Chinook salmon kills from dense phytoplankton bloom
mitigation_methods: NZ$17 million total loss; first NZ record of H. akashiwo
column73: First record of Heterosigma in New Zealand; gill damage and asphyxiation mechanism confirmed

human_cases: Nearly two-month bloom; massive fish mortality
mitigation_attempted: USD 69.5M in aquaculture losses; largest C. polykrikoides event in Korea
column72: First major M. polykrikoides bloom in Korea; recurred almost annually thereafter

human_cases: 75% of farmed tuna stock killed; storm exacerbated bloom effects
mitigation_attempted: AUD 45M loss; industry subsequently relocated to deeper offshore waters
column72: Bloom at 66,000 cells/L; triggered relocation of all tuna farms to offshore sectors

human_deaths: Mass mortality of yellowtail in southern Yatsushiro Sea
mitigation_methods: 5.3 billion JPY; combined 2009-2010 losses exceeded ¥8 billion
column73: Chattonella blooms shifted from Seto Inland Sea to Yatsushiro Sea in recent decades

human_deaths: Most severe K. mikimotoi damage ever recorded; bloom covered >300 km²
mitigation_methods: 2.01 billion CNY (~$330M USD); massive abalone mortality
column73: Largest K. mikimotoi economic loss worldwide; Fujian produces >75% of China's farmed abalone

human_cases: Losses >5,000 tonnes in Comau fjord area; some estimates cite >6,000 t
column73: Intense and widespread H. akashiwo bloom; among the largest in Chilean Patagonian fjords

column72: Recurring M. polykrikoides event; much smaller than 1995

human_cases: Major fishery closures; no direct fish mortality reported
ecological_impact_level: Domoic acid contamination of shellfish; no major human illness outbreak
mitigation_attempted: $97.5M in lost Dungeness crab landings alone; $40M in tourism losses in WA
column72: Driven by unprecedented marine heatwave; Dungeness crab and razor clam fisheries closed
